skill.name: nano-banana-2
skill.description: Generate and edit images with Google Gemini 3.1 Flash Image Preview through the inference.sh CLI.
skill.summary: Run Nano Banana 2 image generation and editing jobs through inference.sh.
skill.author: inference-shell

security_audit.summary: Static analysis correctly detected extensive external command examples and hardcoded network references, but these are expected for a skill that documents inference.sh image generation. The weak cryptography alerts are false positives from ordinary documentation text, and no prompt injection or malicious exfiltration intent was found.

medium_findings.title: External CLI Execution Required
medium_findings.description: The skill is built around running the infsh CLI from Bash examples. This is legitimate for the stated purpose, but it means generated prompts and image references are passed to an external command and service.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documented commands consistently invoke infsh for image generation and related skill installation. The commands are not obfuscated, but the external execution path is central to the skill.

medium_findings.title: Network Submission of Prompt and Image Data
medium_findings.description: The skill references inference.sh documentation and shows image input by URL, with optional Google Search grounding. Users should expect prompts, image URLs, and generated outputs to be handled by third-party services.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network behavior is explicit in the service URL, documentation links, and URL-based image input example. No evidence found that secrets or local files are exfiltrated.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The static high-risk weak cryptography matches occur in descriptive text such as model names, design prompts, and table labels. No cryptographic API, hash function, cipher configuration, or key handling code was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line review shows ordinary Markdown prose and examples, not crypto implementation. The scanner likely matched substrings inside non-security words.

dangerous_patterns.title: Bash Command Examples for infsh
dangerous_patterns.description: Multiple Markdown examples instruct the assistant or user to run infsh commands. This is expected, but it should remain scoped to the allowed infsh command surface.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command examples are direct and repeated. The risk is moderate because execution is intentional and scoped to infsh.

dangerous_patterns.title: Third-Party Service and URL Inputs
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill depends on inference.sh and supports image inputs by URL. Sensitive prompts or private image links could be exposed if users provide them.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The URLs and service dependency are explicit. No hidden destination or suspicious domain was found.

content.user_title: Generate Gemini Images with Nano Banana 2
content.value_statement: Teams need a direct way to create and edit images from an AI assistant. This skill provides ready-to-run inference.sh commands for Gemini 3.1 Flash Image Preview.
actual_capabilities: Provides infsh CLI examples for Gemini 3.1 Flash Image Preview.
Documents text-to-image generation with prompt-based inputs.
Shows image editing with external image URLs as inputs.
Lists options for image count, aspect ratio, resolution, format, and Google Search grounding.
Includes Python SDK examples for standard and streaming runs.
limitations: Requires the inference.sh CLI or Python SDK and a valid login.
Sends prompts and image references to external inference services.
Does not validate user prompts, URLs, or generated image content.
Model availability, pricing, and output behavior depend on inference.sh and Google.

use_cases.title: Create Campaign Visuals
use_cases.description: Generate draft images in several styles and aspect ratios for ads, posts, or landing pages.

use_cases.title: Prototype Product Concepts
use_cases.description: Turn short creative briefs into image options for early review and iteration.

use_cases.title: Automate Image Generation Tests
use_cases.description: Use CLI and SDK examples to test prompts, resolution settings, and streaming output.

prompt_templates.title: Generate a Simple Image
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Nano Banana 2 to generate one photorealistic image of a modern desk setup in natural light.

prompt_templates.title: Create Multiple Variations
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Nano Banana 2 to create four square logo concepts for a neighborhood bakery with a clean visual style.

prompt_templates.title: Edit an Existing Image
prompt_templates.scenario: Apply a requested change to an input image
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Nano Banana 2 to edit this image URL by adding warm evening light and keeping the subject unchanged.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Grounded Visual Brief
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Nano Banana 2 with Google Search grounding to visualize current weather in Tokyo as a 16:9 editorial illustration.

output_examples.input: Prompt for a photorealistic banana floating in space.
output_examples.output: A generated image result with a short model description and output metadata.

output_examples.input: Prompt to add a rainbow to a supplied landscape image URL.
output_examples.output: An edited image result that keeps the landscape and adds the requested sky detail.

output_examples.input: Prompt for four minimalist coffee shop logo options.
output_examples.output: Several generated logo concepts returned with generation metadata for review.

best_practices: Use clear prompts that specify subject, style, composition, lighting, and output size.
Avoid private image URLs or confidential prompts unless external processing is approved.
Start with 1K drafts before requesting higher resolution outputs.
anti_patterns: Do not paste API keys, credentials, or private customer data into image prompts.
Do not assume external image URLs are safe to process without permission.
Do not request final production assets without reviewing model outputs for accuracy and rights concerns.

faq.question: What does this skill use to generate images?
faq.answer: It uses the inference.sh CLI or Python SDK to run Google Gemini 3.1 Flash Image Preview.

faq.question: Can it edit existing images?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill documents image editing through input image URLs and a prompt describing the requested change.

faq.question: Does it support multiple generated images?
faq.answer: Yes. The documented input options include a number of images parameter.

faq.question: Can it use current information?
faq.answer: Yes. The examples include an option to enable Google Search grounding for current context.

faq.question: Is it safe for confidential content?
faq.answer: Use caution. Prompts and image references are processed by external services, so sensitive content needs approval first.

faq.question: Does the skill install inference.sh?
faq.answer: No. It points to installation instructions and assumes the required CLI or SDK is available.
